mnemonics.title: OS FUNCTIONS MNEMONIC
mnemonics.content: MAIN FUNCTIONS OF OS: 'MR PIE' = MANAGEMENT (Resources), PROTECTION, INTERFACE (User), EXECUTION (Programs). Alternatively: 'SERENA' = Security, Execution, Resource Management, Error Detection, Networking, Accounting.

mnemonics.title: OS COMPONENTS MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: KEY COMPONENTS: 'KSDSU' = KERNEL, Shell, Device Drivers, System Utilities, User Interface. Remember: 'KEEP SYSTEM DEVICES USABLE'.

mnemonics.title: OS EVOLUTION ORDER
mnemonics.content: Evolution sequence: MANUAL (operators) -> BATCH (cards/tapes) -> MULTIPROGRAMMED (memory) -> TIME-SHARING (interactive) -> PERSONAL (single user) -> DISTRIBUTED (networked) -> MOBILE (touch). Remember: 'MANY BATCHES TASTE DELICIOUSLY IN MORNING'.

mnemonics.title: SYSTEM CALL TYPES
mnemonics.content: System call categories: 'PFCD' = Process control, File management, Device management, Communication, Information maintenance. Remember: 'PROCESS FILES DAILY CONSCIOUSLY'.

acronyms.usage: Software that manages computer hardware and provides services to applications
Set of functions and procedures for building software applications
Visual interface with icons, windows that users interact with
Text-based interface where users type commands
Firmware that initializes hardware during boot process
Set of functions and procedures for building software applications
Volatile memory that stores data temporarily during execution
Non-volatile memory containing permanent boot instructions

visualTips.visualization: Think of a building with a RESTRICTED AREA (kernel mode - full access) and PUBLIC AREA (user mode - limited access). Only authorized personnel (OS functions) can enter restricted areas.

Imagine turning on a light switch: Power flows through wires (boot program loads) -> Main switch turns on (kernel loads) -> Room becomes functional (OS ready). The bootstrap is the initial power surge that starts everything.
Think of a professor (CPU) with multiple students (programs) waiting. While one student asks a question (I/O wait), the professor helps others. This maximizes the professor's productive time.
A RECIPE (program) is instructions on paper, while COOKING (process) is actively using those instructions with ingredients, stove, and actions. Same recipe, different states.

Think of SYSTEM CALLS as the RECEPTION DESK in a hotel. Guests (applications) cannot go directly to all areas; they request services through reception (system calls) which coordinates with housekeeping (OS kernel).

analogies.analogy: Just as a government manages resources (roads, hospitals, schools) for citizens while providing services and maintaining order, an OS manages hardware resources for applications while providing services and protection.
Like a FLIGHT CONTROL TOWER manages runways, gates, and planes, the OS manages CPU time, memory allocation, and device access to ensure smooth operations.
The kernel is like the BRAIN of the operating system - it controls all essential functions, processes information, and coordinates between different body parts (hardware components).
The shell acts like a WAITER in a restaurant - takes your order (commands), communicates it to the kitchen (kernel), and brings back the prepared food (results).
